content.problemStatement:
The Little Elephant from the Zoo of Lviv currently is on the military mission. There are **N** enemy buildings placed in a row and numbered from left to right strating from **0**. Each building **i** (except the first and the last) has exactly two adjacent buildings with indices **i-1** and **i+1**. The first and the last buildings have just a single adjacent building.


Some of the buildings contain bombs. When bomb explodes in some building it destroys it and all adjacent to it buildings.


You are given the string **S** of length **N**, where **Si** is **1** if the **i**-th building contains bomb, **0** otherwise. Find for the Little Elephant the number of buildings that will not be destroyed after all bombs explode. Please note that all bombs explode simultaneously.
constraints: S consists only of digits 0 and 1
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains a single integer N - the number of buildings. The second line contains a string S of length N, where Si is 1 if the i-th building contains a bomb, 0 otherwise.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of buildings that will not be destroyed after all bombs explode.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the building at index 1 contains a bomb, so it and its adjacent buildings (at indices 0 and 2) are destroyed. For the second test case, the building at index 0 contains a bomb, so it and its adjacent building (at index 1) are destroyed. The building at index 4 also contains a bomb, so it and its adjacent building (at index 3) are destroyed. For the third test case, there are no bombs, so no buildings are destroyed.
The buildings at indices 0, 1, and 4 contain bombs, so all buildings are destroyed.
There is no bomb, so the building is not destroyed.

solution.approach: The key insight is to iterate through the string S and mark the buildings that will be destroyed by a bomb. A building will be destroyed if it contains a bomb or if it is adjacent to a building that contains a bomb. We can use a boolean array to keep track of the destroyed buildings. Finally, we count the number of buildings that are not destroyed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N = int(input())
    S = input()
    destroyed = [False] * N
    for i in range(N):
        if S[i] == '1':
            if i - 1 >= 0: destroyed[i - 1] = True
            destroyed[i] = True
            if i + 1 < N: destroyed[i + 1] = True
    count = sum(1 for i in range(N) if not destroyed[i])
    print(count)
